hints.Herodis: 'Herodis' is a proper noun meaning 'of Herod'

hints.present indicative: Present of 'esse' is 'est'; 'fuit' is the perfect stem

hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'fio' (to become)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the principal parts of 'fugio' (to flee)
hints.fundo, fundere, fudi, fusum: These are the principal parts of 'fundo' (to pour)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'dies'; 'diebus' with -bus is ablative/dative
hints.dative: The -bus ending can be dative, but after 'in' it is ablative of time

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'dies' has genitive 'diei'
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'dies' has genitive 'diei'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has -us in the genitive; 'dies' has genitive 'diei' (5th declension)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Herodes'; 'Herodis' with -is is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'Herodes' has genitive 'Herodis'
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'Herodes' follows Greek 3rd declension
hints.indeclinable: 'Herodes' does decline: Herodes, Herodis, Herodi, Herodem, Herode

translation: There was in the days of Herod
